title: To Lose Thee, Sweeter Than To Gain
content:
To lose thee, sweeter than to gain
    All other hearts I knew.
    'T is true the drought is destitute,
    But then I had the dew!

    The Caspian has its realms of sand,
    Its other realm of sea;
    Without the sterile perquisite
    No Caspian could be.
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "To Lose Thee, Sweeter Than To Gain"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
